title: What are Structs?
description: Group several related variables together into one place.

slides.title: Structures 🏢
slides.content:
A structure (or **struct**) is a way to group several related variables into one single place.

Each variable in the structure is known as a "member" of the structure.

slides.title: Structs vs. Arrays 🥊
slides.content: Unlike arrays, which can only contain one single data type (`int` array, `string` array), a struct can actually contain **many different data types** bundled together!

quizData.question: What is the primary difference between a Struct and an Array?

options.text: A Struct can bundle completely different data types (strings, ints, bools) together at once inside one variable

quizData.explanation: Structs form "objects" that can hold multiple properties like age `int` and name `string` concurrently.

slides.title: Creating a Struct 🏗️
slides.content:
To create a structure, use the `struct` keyword and declare each of its members inside curly braces.

After the closing brace, you can optionally instantiate instances of your new structure.

quizData.question: The variables housed inside a struct block are officially called 'members'.
quizData.explanation: Yes, we refer to them as 'members' of the overarching structure.
